output.description: Applied type guard to PostSelectorSortable onChange handler to ensure value is always coerced to number[] before assigning to selectedPosts attribute

filesModified.changeDescription: Added Array.isArray() type guard with falsy check to safely coerce PostSelectorSortable's `number | number[]` return value into `number[]`

warnings: entrypoint size limit: js/editor (328 KiB), js/screen (363 KiB), css/screen (262 KiB)
webpack performance recommendations: use import() or require.ensure for code splitting
buildStatus.notes: Build completed successfully. Performance warnings are pre-existing and not related to this change.

typeGuardPattern.rationale: PostSelectorSortable returns `number | number[]`. In multi-select mode (no limit prop), it typically returns number[], but TypeScript requires handling both cases. The type guard ensures: (1) if value is already an array, pass through; (2) if value is a number, wrap in array; (3) if value is falsy (0 or undefined), return empty array.
typeGuardPattern.adaptedFrom: blocks/FeaturedPost/FeaturedPost.tsx lines 146-151 (inverse pattern for single-select mode)

testingNotes.reason: This is a pure TypeScript type safety fix. The PostSelectorSortable component already returns arrays in multi-select mode at runtime. The type guard satisfies the TypeScript compiler without changing runtime behavior.
verificationSteps: Manual verification suggested: Test block editor with manual post selection mode
Manual verification suggested: Verify selectedPosts attribute saves correctly as array

testimonialsCarousel.note: TestimonialsCarousel.tsx has the exact same PostSelectorSortable type issue at lines 160-167. Will be fixed in a separate pipeline as stated in the planning output.

fixesApplied.description: Added type guard to PostSelectorSortable onChange handler
